summary: Matched the visual-effects units `eflib`, new `eflib_alloc`, and `ef_061D`, moving them to `Object(Matching, ...)` in `configure.py`. The PR also corrected effect/particle symbol names and section boundaries, split the effect object allocator BSS out of `eflib.c`, cleaned up related headers/types, and made small baselib particle/psappsrt signature and static-symbol adjustments needed by the match. decomp.dev reported +24,216 matched code bytes, +25,828 linked code bytes, and 45 new matches, with a small remaining regression in unmatched `efAsync_80063930`.
what_changed: `configure.py` changed `melee/ef/eflib.c` and `melee/ef/ef_061D.c` from `NonMatching` to `Matching`, and added `melee/ef/eflib_alloc.c` as a matching object.
Added a new `eflib_alloc.c`/`eflib_alloc.h` unit for `efLib_AllocData`, with `splits.txt` assigning `.bss start:0x80458EB0 end:0x80458EDC`; `eflib.c` BSS now starts at `0x80458EE0`.

Implemented `ef_061D.c` from an empty file to a 518-line matched effect dispatch function centered on `ef_80061D70`, using a large `gfx_id` switch and calls into `efLib_*`, `hsd_8039EFAC`, and JObj rotation helpers.
Reworked `eflib.c` heavily, with decomp.dev listing many newly 100% matched `efLib_*` functions and matched `.data`, `.sdata`, and `.sdata2` for the unit.

Renamed effect symbols to match ownership/function purpose, including `Effect_RemoveUserData` to `efLib_RemoveUserData`, `fn_8005C904` to `efLib_RenderCallback`, and `Effect_AllocData` to `efLib_AllocData`.
Adjusted symbol scopes and sizes for many effect strings/constants from global to local in `symbols.txt`, and refined `splits.txt` section endpoints for `eflib`, `ef_061D`, and `efasync`.
Updated `dballoc.c` to include `ef/eflib_alloc.h` and to use `efLib_AllocData` for object-allocation limit toggling instead of the old `Effect_AllocData` name.
Cleaned up baselib particle naming by renaming static `ptclref` to address-qualified `ptclref_804D0E5C` in `particle.static.h`, `particle.c`, and `symbols.txt`.
Changed `psAddParticleAppSRT_begin` from the placeholder name `HSD_PSAppSrt_803A425C`, and changed its signature to return `HSD_psAppSRT*` while assigning `pp->appsrt`.

Simplified/rewrote parts of `psstructs.h`, including a formatted `HSD_Particle` layout, typedef `ef_Symbols` with `STATIC_ASSERT(sizeof(ef_Symbols) == 0xC)`, and an asserted `efAsync_80067980_objalloc_t` size of `0x24`.
smart_moves: Isolating `efLib_AllocData` into `eflib_alloc.c` made the allocator BSS a separately matched object instead of leaving it embedded in `eflib.c`; this also let `dballoc.c` depend on a small allocator header rather than the whole effect library header.
The PR matched data as well as code by fixing section boundaries, symbol sizes, alignment, and local/global scope; the decomp.dev report shows matched data also improved rather than only text.
Renaming placeholder/legacy symbols to subsystem-owned names (`efLib_*`, `psAddParticleAppSRT_begin`, `ptclref_804D0E5C`) made cross-file dependencies clearer and reduced ambiguous generic names.
Adding `STATIC_ASSERT`s for inferred particle/effect structs kept header cleanup tied to layout evidence, which is important when matching code that is sensitive to offsets.
The `ef_061D` implementation preserved varargs behavior through a dedicated `EF061D_VA_ARG(t)` macro, avoiding repeated low-level `__va_arg` boilerplate in a large switch.
decomp_lessons: For Melee effect work, allocator data may belong in its own object split even if adjacent to a larger library in memory; check `.bss` ranges before assuming ownership by the nearest C file.
When a match requires data fixes, update `symbols.txt` scopes/sizes and `splits.txt` endpoints together; code matching alone can leave data accounting wrong.
Headers used by unrelated systems should expose the narrowest symbol needed: `dballoc.c` only needed the effect allocator, so a dedicated `eflib_alloc.h` was preferable to including `eflib.h`.
Large effect dispatchers using varargs can be matched by centralizing typed vararg extraction in a macro and keeping argument consumption order explicit per switch case.
Baselib support structs such as `HSD_Particle` should keep offset comments and layout assertions because downstream effect matches depend on precise field offsets.
Address-suffixed names such as `ptclref_804D0E5C` are acceptable when a more semantic name is not yet proven and help distinguish local static data in generated symbol tables.
Changing a helper's return type can be part of a match if call sites consume the assigned object; here `psAddParticleAppSRT_begin` returns the assigned `HSD_psAppSRT*`.
naming_conventions: Effect library globals/functions use the `efLib_` prefix, e.g. `efLib_AllocData`, `efLib_RemoveUserData`, and `efLib_RenderCallback`.
File/module-specific unresolved data keeps module prefix plus address, e.g. `efLib_803BF7A0`, `ef_803BF9D0`, and `ptclref_804D0E5C`.
Particle SRT helper names use descriptive `ps*` prefixes and `_begin` suffix for begin/attach setup helpers, e.g. `psAddParticleAppSRT_begin` and `psAddGeneratorAppSRT_begin`.
Keep function names in symbol files synchronized with header prototypes when replacing placeholder names such as `fn_8005C904` or `HSD_PSAppSrt_803A425C`.
Prefer subsystem ownership in names over generic `Effect_*` when the symbol is specifically part of `eflib`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: `ef_061D.c` uses `#define EF061D_VA_ARG(t) (*((t*) __va_arg(vlist_arg, _var_arg_typeof(t))))` to reproduce compiler vararg access patterns.
`ef_80061D70` includes `PAD_STACK(80)`, indicating stack-shape padding was needed for the match.
The implementation uses explicit double constants for ±1.5707963267948966 before assigning to `f32` rotation values, preserving the original conversion pattern.
Data matching relied on converting many constants/strings from global to local symbol scope and correcting exact object sizes/alignments in `symbols.txt`.
Matched-object registration in `configure.py` was updated only after the object split and section ranges were corrected.
The PR used layout assertions in headers (`STATIC_ASSERT`) to lock inferred struct/object sizes while matching code that indexes fields by offset.
review_feedback: No formal review comments were present in the slice.
The author noted in the PR body that they had not done this in a while and asked reviewers to flag anything not kosher.
A decomp.dev bot report showed 45 new matches and one small unmatched regression in `main/melee/ef/efasync` item `efAsync_80063930` (-22 bytes, 98.61% to 98.46%).
The author later commented: "womp womp, will fix tomorrow"; the slice does not identify the exact issue being acknowledged.
follow_up_queries: Inspect later PRs by Savestate2A03 touching `efsync` or `efasync`, since this PR body says those were being worked on separately.
Search for subsequent changes to `ef_061D.c` cleanup because the added file contains the comment `WIP CLEAN THIS UP AND SYNC`.
Check whether the decomp.dev-reported `efAsync_80063930` regression was fixed in a later effect PR.
Look for future renames of remaining address-based `efLib_*` and `ef_*` data symbols once semantics are known.
Compare later `psstructs.h` edits to see whether the `HSD_Particle` layout and `ef_Symbols` typedef stabilized.
